pattern: The string for level n is built from the one at level n-1 with a single '1' dropped in the middle, so a query for position k either lands on that middle bit, falls in the untouched left half, or falls in the mirrored-and-flipped right half -- each case reduces to a smaller version of the same question.
hints: The length of the level-n string is always 2^n - 1, and the middle position is a '1' by construction.
If k lands exactly on the middle, you're done. If it's before the middle, the left half is just a verbatim copy of the level-(n-1) string, so recurse with the same k.
If k is after the middle, that half is the level-(n-1) string reversed and with every bit flipped -- so recurse on the mirrored index into level n-1, then flip whatever bit comes back.

edge_inputs.description: smallest possible level, only one bit
exactly the middle position of level 5, always a 1
last bit of level 5, in the flipped-and-mirrored half

stress.description: random level and a random valid position within it
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 20)
    length = (1 << n) - 1
    k = rng.randint(1, length)
    return [n, k]
